title: Change the location of Mandarin 3 — schedule swaps + Ms. Mac
html:
TL;DR

  • Five-line August 22, 2019 schedule-shopping note (back-to-school week): change the location of Mandarin 3, swap AP US for Ms. Mac, swap basic design for a programming class, watershed for astrology, ask what they offer instead of storytelling.
  • Earliest documented mention of Yahir actively swapping into a programming class — the engineering-curiosity vector is on record by August 2019, predating any Rankett/Brotein code by 5+ years.
  • Documents the Ms. Mac teacher preference for AP US History — the same APUSH that surfaces in the December Brenda todo, suggesting the swap landed.
  • "Watershed for astrology" — Yahir is willing to drop an environmental-science elective to take astrology — a fingerprint of the curiosity-over-rigor instinct that reappears in the 6 month adventure manifesto.
  • The note is a clean schedule-as-portfolio decision — Yahir treating his senior-year course load like an allocation problem, three days into the school year.

Notes

The note documents the high-school equivalent of portfolio reallocation: drop what isn't compounding, swap into adjacent surface area, locate the right teacher. Mandarin 3 needs to change location (the room or the period is wrong); AP US needs Ms. Mac not whoever is currently slotted (teacher selection beats course selection); basic design gets replaced with programming (skill-building over aesthetics). The whole list is a junior analyst's portfolio note, three days in.

The swap-watershed-for-astrology decision is the most teenage line in the note. Yahir is signaling that the actual environmental-science class isn't paying off and he'd rather take a fringe-curiosity slot. The same instinct shows up in the 6 month adventure decision to walk away from a stable job for novelty.

The "what do they offer instead of storytelling" line suggests storytelling was a class he'd considered and is now looking for the equivalent — the always-have-a-replacement-ready habit. This is the same brain that, in 2025, ranks coffee shops by Wi-Fi and outlet count before committing to a workday venue.

Privacy note

Ms. Mac is a teacher abbreviation, non-identifying. No redactions needed.

Connections

  • 2019 08 26 dream of dads key — same-week sibling note
  • 2019 12 12 talk to brenda — the APUSH that landed (with Ms. Mac per this note)
  • grants pass teen era · grants pass or
  • mandarin 3 · apush — schedule artifacts
  • disconnected from outcome · asking the right question
